title: Hansbeke

is a village and *[deelgemeente](/wiki/Deelgemeente "Deelgemeente")* (sub-municipality) in the municipality of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze") in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). The village is located about 13 kilometres (8.1 mi) west of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

**Hansbeke** is a village and *[deelgemeente](/wiki/Deelgemeente "Deelgemeente")* (sub-municipality) in the municipality of [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze") in the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"). The village is located about 13 kilometres (8.1 mi) west of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hansbeke&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The village was first mentioned in 1147 as Hansbeka, and means "brook near [Hamme](/wiki/Hamme,_Belgium "Hamme, Belgium")". The parish was demarcated in 1242, and became part of the [Diocese of Ghent](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Ghent "Roman Catholic Diocese of Ghent") in 1559. In 1838, a railway station opened on the [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") to [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges") railway line. Later the [E40](/wiki/European_route_E40 "European route E40") highway was constructed parallel to the railway line. The improved connection to the outside world resulted in the transformation of an agricultural community into a commuter's town.[[3]](#cite_note-plat-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Hansbeke was an independent municipality until 1977, when it was merged to [Nevele](/wiki/Nevele "Nevele").[[5]](#cite_note-5) In 2019, it was merged in [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze"). The villagers have established their own village council in December 2019, because they were worried that they were being ignored by the city in the larger municipality.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Buildings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hansbeke&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Buildings")]

The St Peter and Paul Church was first mentioned in 1147. The old church was damaged by a storm in 1780, and demolished in 1790. In 1793, a three aisled basilica-like church was constructed. The tower was destroyed in 1918, and rebuilt between 1922 and 1923 by [Valentin Vaerwyck](/wiki/Valentin_Vaerwyck "Valentin Vaerwyck") in neo-Flemish Renaissance style.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[3]](#cite_note-plat-3)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hansbeke&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Erwin Mortier](/wiki/Erwin_Mortier "Erwin Mortier") (born 1965), author[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* [Julius Nieuwland](/wiki/Julius_Nieuwland "Julius Nieuwland") (1899–1985), priest and professor of chemistry and botany[[9]](#cite_note-9)
